data.title: Is ING (ENXTAM:INGA) Using Buybacks And Board Changes To Redefine Its Capital Allocation Story?
data.description:
• ING Groep NV has continued its €1.10 billion share buyback programme, repurchasing 1,907,413 shares in the week of 19–23 January 2026, while also preparing to report its Q4 2025 results on 29 January 2026 and confirming the planned 2026 departure of Supervisory Board member Herna Verhagen.
  • Together, the ongoing capital return through buybacks, upcoming earnings release, and forthcoming change on the Supervisory Board highlight how ING is balancing shareholder focus with governance transition.
  • Against this backdrop, we’ll examine how ING’s continued share buyback activity shapes the company’s investment narrative for investors.

We've found 13 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

What Is ING Groep's Investment Narrative?

To stay invested in ING Groep, you really have to believe in its appeal as a steady European bank with disciplined capital return and measured growth rather than spectacular expansion. The ongoing €1.10 billion share buyback, now nearly half complete, reinforces that story by shrinking share capital alongside a pattern of special and interim dividends. The short term catalyst in focus is the upcoming Q4 2025 earnings release, which may either support or challenge expectations around earnings growth and return on equity that remains in the low double digits. Herna Verhagen’s planned 2026 departure from the Supervisory Board looks more like a manageable governance transition than a shock, though investors will watch closely who takes over her remuneration and risk oversight roles. Overall, the latest announcements appear directionally consistent with ING’s recent performance and recent share price strength, without materially changing the main upside drivers, while existing issues such as loan loss coverage and valuation versus peers stay front of mind.

However, one risk in particular could matter more than it first appears. ING Groep's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Eleven fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly €18.23 to about €50.04 per share, showing how far apart private investors can be. Set this against ING’s continued buyback and upcoming Q4 2025 results, and you start to see why differing views on earnings quality, capital strength and governance could all influence how the story unfolds from here.

Explore 11 other fair value estimates on ING Groep - why the stock might be worth 27% less than the current price!

Disagree with this assessment? Create your own narrative in under 3 minutes - extraordinary investment returns rarely come from following the herd.

  • A great starting point for your ING Groep research is our analysis highlighting 3 key rewards and 2 important warning signs that could impact your investment decision.
  • Our free ING Groep research report provides a comprehensive fundamental analysis summarized in a single visual - the Snowflake - making it easy to evaluate ING Groep's overall financial health at a glance.

  • Uncover the next big thing with financially sound penny stocks that balance risk and reward.

data.title: Ubtech’s RMB 300 Million Robotics Fund Commitment Could Be A Game Changer For Ubtech Robotics (SEHK:9880)
data.description:
• In January 2026, Ubtech Robotics entered into a Partnership Agreement with Liuzhou Industrial Holding Fund and Liuzhou Industrial Guidance Fund to create a RMB 660 million investment vehicle focused on the intelligent robot industry chain around Liuzhou City’s industrial base.
  • By committing RMB 300 million alongside two major local funds, Ubtech is helping build an ecosystem that targets upstream components, intelligent infrastructure, and broader AI and robotics development through portfolio investments.
  • Next, we’ll examine how Ubtech’s RMB 300 million commitment to this intelligent robotics investment fund could shape its broader investment narrative.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 111 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Is Ubtech Robotics' Investment Narrative?

To own Ubtech Robotics, you have to believe that its heavy investment in AI and humanoid robotics can ultimately justify a very rich sales multiple, despite sizeable and ongoing losses. The new Liuzhou partnership fits that story by giving Ubtech exposure to upstream components and intelligent infrastructure, which could support product development and supply chain depth over time, but it is unlikely to shift the near term picture of high cash burn and shareholder dilution on its own. With the share price already reflecting very high growth expectations and the company still forecast to be loss making, the more immediate catalysts remain product adoption, margin progress and funding visibility, while key risks cluster around execution, dilution and governance after a period of rapid board turnover.

However, one risk around ongoing dilution and capital needs is something investors should not ignore. The valuation report we've compiled suggests that Ubtech Robotics' current price could be inflated.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Across three Simply Wall St Community fair value views, estimates span from around HK$21 to roughly HK$157 per share, underlining how differently people are weighing Ubtech’s rich valuation against persistent losses and funding needs, and why it helps to compare several independent viewpoints before forming a view.

data.title: Tohoku Steel (TSE:5484) Margin Improvement Supports Bullish Earnings Quality Narrative
data.description:
Advertisement

Q3 2026 earnings snapshot

Tohoku Steel (TSE:5484) has reported Q3 2026 revenue of ¥5,223 million, Basic EPS of ¥50.22 and net income of ¥373 million, with the latest twelve month figures showing Basic EPS of ¥157.07 and net income of ¥1,168 million on revenue of ¥20.38 billion. The company has seen quarterly revenue move between ¥5,028 million and ¥5,349 million over the past six reported periods, while quarterly Basic EPS has ranged from ¥24.57 to ¥50.22. This places margin quality and earnings consistency at the center of investor attention.

See our full analysis for Tohoku Steel.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to line them up against the widely followed narratives around Tohoku Steel to see which views the recent results support and which ones they start to question.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

5.7% net margin points to firmer profitability

  • Over the last 12 months, Tohoku Steel converted ¥20,375 million of revenue into ¥1,168 million of net income, giving a 5.7% net profit margin compared with 4.0% in the prior year.
  • What stands out for the bullish narrative is that this higher margin sits alongside trailing Basic EPS of ¥157.07, which is above each of the last six individual quarterly EPS prints, even though quarterly revenue has been tightly clustered between ¥5,028 million and ¥5,349 million.
    • This combination of a 5.7% margin and 35.5% earnings growth over the last year is being used to support the bullish view that recent profitability is not just a one off but part of a stronger trailing earnings profile.
    • At the same time, the five year annualized earnings growth of 4.2% gives bulls and cautious investors a clear contrast between the recent 35.5% jump and the longer run pace, which is a key point for anyone testing how durable this margin profile might be.

14.1x P/E and ¥3,880.64 DCF fair value

  • The shares trade on a 14.1x P/E, a touch below both the JP Metals & Mining industry average of 14.4x and the peer average of 14.9x, while the current price of ¥2,213 sits under a stated DCF fair value of ¥3,880.64.
  • For investors leaning toward a bullish stance, this gap between the ¥2,213 share price and the DCF fair value of ¥3,880.64, together with the slightly lower P/E than peers, is often interpreted as valuation headroom rather than a warning sign.
    • The description of the company as having high quality trailing earnings, alongside 35.5% year over year earnings growth, gives bulls specific numbers to point to when they argue that a 14.1x multiple does not look stretched.
    • On the other hand, anyone cautious can compare that DCF fair value with the longer run 4.2% annualized earnings growth and ask whether the strong recent year is enough to justify the full implied upside, which is where bullish and more conservative views tend to differ.
📊 Read the full Tohoku Steel Consensus Narrative.

Quarterly EPS swings within steady revenue band

  • Over the last six reported periods, revenue stayed in a narrow range around ¥5,028 million to ¥5,349 million, while Basic EPS moved between ¥24.57 and ¥50.22, including the latest ¥50.22 in Q3 2026 on net income of ¥373 million.
  • General market opinion often focuses on how much earnings can move even when the top line is relatively steady, and Tohoku Steel offers a clear example of that pattern.
    • With trailing twelve month revenue around ¥20,375 million and net income of ¥1,168 million, the 35.5% earnings growth figure sits alongside only modest movement in reported sales, which is a key detail for anyone thinking about what is driving EPS changes.
    • Investors who prefer a cleaner earnings story will probably pay close attention to how often EPS lands near the upper end of that ¥24.57 to ¥50.22 range, given that the trailing EPS of ¥157.07 is built from those more volatile quarterly building blocks.

Next Steps

Don't just look at this quarter; the real story is in the long-term trend. We've done an in-depth analysis on Tohoku Steel's growth and its valuation to see if today's price is a bargain. Add the company to your watchlist or portfolio now so you don't miss the next big move.

See What Else Is Out There

Tohoku Steel’s tightly clustered revenue, along with noticeably swinging quarterly EPS, highlights how much of the current story rests on earnings that are not consistently smooth.

If you prefer companies where both the top line and bottom line move in a steadier pattern, check out CTA_SCREENER_STABLE_GROWTH to focus on ideas built around more predictable growth and potentially less earnings noise.

data.title: Brambles Limited's (ASX:BXB) Popularity With Investors Is Under Threat From Overpricing
data.description:
With a price-to-earnings (or "P/E") ratio of 25.2x Brambles Limited (ASX:BXB) may be sending bearish signals at the moment, given that almost half of all companies in Australia have P/E ratios under 21x and even P/E's lower than 12x are not unusual. Nonetheless, we'd need to dig a little deeper to determine if there is a rational basis for the elevated P/E.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Brambles' earnings growth of late has been pretty similar to most other companies. One possibility is that the P/E is high because investors think this modest earnings performance will accelerate. If not, then existing shareholders may be a little nervous about the viability of the share price.

Check out our latest analysis for Brambles

If you'd like to see what analysts are forecasting going forward, you should check out our free report on Brambles.
Advertisement

How Is Brambles' Growth Trending?

The only time you'd be truly comfortable seeing a P/E as high as Brambles' is when the company's growth is on track to outshine the market.

Taking a look back first, we see that the company managed to grow earnings per share by a handy 12% last year. The latest three year period has also seen an excellent 57% overall rise in EPS, aided somewhat by its short-term performance. Therefore, it's fair to say the earnings growth recently has been superb for the company.

Shifting to the future, estimates from the analysts covering the company suggest earnings should grow by 11% per annum over the next three years. That's shaping up to be materially lower than the 17% per year growth forecast for the broader market.

In light of this, it's alarming that Brambles' P/E sits above the majority of other companies. Apparently many investors in the company are way more bullish than analysts indicate and aren't willing to let go of their stock at any price. Only the boldest would assume these prices are sustainable as this level of earnings growth is likely to weigh heavily on the share price eventually.

The Key Takeaway

Typically, we'd caution against reading too much into price-to-earnings ratios when settling on investment decisions, though it can reveal plenty about what other market participants think about the company.

Our examination of Brambles' analyst forecasts revealed that its inferior earnings outlook isn't impacting its high P/E anywhere near as much as we would have predicted. Right now we are increasingly uncomfortable with the high P/E as the predicted future earnings aren't likely to support such positive sentiment for long. Unless these conditions improve markedly, it's very challenging to accept these prices as being reasonable.

Before you settle on your opinion, we've discovered 2 warning signs for Brambles that you should be aware of.

Of course, you might find a fantastic investment by looking at a few good candidates. So take a peek at this free list of companies with a strong growth track record, trading on a low P/E.

data.title: Island Pharmaceuticals Limited's (ASX:ILA) Profit Outlook
data.description:
With the business potentially at an important milestone, we thought we'd take a closer look at Island Pharmaceuticals Limited's (ASX:ILA) future prospects. Island Pharmaceuticals Limited, a drug repurposing company, focuses on the development of antiviral therapeutics for antiviral therapeutics in Australia and the United States of America. On 30 June 2025, the AU$105m market-cap company posted a loss of AU$3.9m for its most recent financial year. Many investors are wondering about the rate at which Island Pharmaceuticals will turn a profit, with the big question being “when will the company breakeven?” We've put together a brief outline of industry analyst expectations for the company, its year of breakeven and its implied growth rate.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Expectations from some of the Australian Pharmaceuticals analysts is that Island Pharmaceuticals is on the verge of breakeven. They expect the company to post a final loss in 2026, before turning a profit of AU$280m in 2027. Therefore, the company is expected to breakeven just over a year from now. In order to meet this breakeven date, we calculated the rate at which the company must grow year-on-year. It turns out an average annual growth rate of 33% is expected, which is rather optimistic! Should the business grow at a slower rate, it will become profitable at a later date than expected.

We're not going to go through company-specific developments for Island Pharmaceuticals given that this is a high-level summary, though, take into account that typically a pharma company has lumpy cash flows which are contingent on the drug and stage of product development the business is in. So, a high growth rate is not out of the ordinary, particularly when a company is in a period of investment.

See our latest analysis for Island Pharmaceuticals

One thing we’d like to point out is that Island Pharmaceuticals has no debt on its balance sheet, which is quite unusual for a cash-burning pharma, which usually has a high level of debt relative to its equity. This means that the company has been operating purely on its equity investment and has no debt burden. This aspect reduces the risk around investing in the loss-making company.

Advertisement

Next Steps:

There are too many aspects of Island Pharmaceuticals to cover in one brief article, but the key fundamentals for the company can all be found in one place – Island Pharmaceuticals' company page on Simply Wall St. We've also compiled a list of important factors you should further examine:

  1. Historical Track Record: What has Island Pharmaceuticals' performance been like over the past? Go into more detail in the past track record analysis and take a look at the free visual representations of our analysis for more clarity.
  2. Management Team: An experienced management team on the helm increases our confidence in the business – take a look at who sits on Island Pharmaceuticals' board and the CEO’s background.
  3. Other High-Performing Stocks: Are there other stocks that provide better prospects with proven track records? Explore our free list of these great stocks here.

data.title: Is Leidos Holdings (LDOS) Still Attractively Priced After A 96% Three-Year Share Price Gain
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Leidos Holdings at around US$187 per share still offers value after a strong run, this article will walk through what the current price might imply.
  • The stock has delivered a 33.6% return over the past year and 96.3% over three years. More recently it has seen a 2.0% decline over 7 days and a 0.7% return over the last month, with a 2.0% return year to date.
  • Investors have been paying close attention to Leidos because of its role as a major government and defense contractor. Recent headlines have focused on its work across national security, civil, and health programs, and the pipeline of large contracts in these areas. This broader context helps frame why the share price has been active and why sentiment around future funding and contract awards matters.
  • Leidos currently has a valuation score of 5 out of 6, which suggests that on several checks the stock screens as undervalued. Next we will look at how traditional methods like P/E multiples and cash flow models line up, before finishing with a more holistic way to think about what that score really means for you.

Leidos Holdings delivered 33.6% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Professional Services industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Leidos Holdings Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today using a required rate of return, giving you an estimate of what the business might be worth per share right now.

For Leidos Holdings, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $1.33b. Analysts provide forecasts out to 2028, and Simply Wall St extends those estimates further using its own assumptions. For example, projected free cash flow is $1.62b in 2026 and $1.71b in 2028, with discounted values provided for each year through 2035 in the model.

When all those projected and discounted cash flows are added together, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $290.87 per share. Compared with the current share price of around $187, this implies an intrinsic discount of roughly 35.7%, which indicates that the shares screen as undervalued on this cash flow view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Leidos Holdings is undervalued by 35.7%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 876 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Leidos Holdings.

Approach 2: Leidos Holdings Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Leidos Holdings, the P/E ratio is a useful way to relate what you pay per share to the earnings the business is currently generating. Investors usually accept a higher P/E when they expect stronger growth or see lower risk, and a lower P/E when they expect slower growth or higher risk.

Leidos trades on a P/E of 17.03x. That sits below the Professional Services industry average P/E of 23.15x and the peer group average of 38.78x. On simple comparisons, the shares look relatively inexpensive compared with both the sector and peers.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Leidos is 24.51x. This is a proprietary estimate of what a more appropriate P/E might be, given the company’s earnings profile, industry, profit margins, market value and risk characteristics. Because it blends these company specific factors, the Fair Ratio can be more tailored than a broad industry or peer average. With the current P/E of 17.03x sitting below the Fair Ratio of 24.51x, Leidos screens as undervalued on this earnings based check.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1422 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Leidos Holdings Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, a simple way to connect your view of Leidos Holdings with the numbers behind it. A Narrative is your story about the company, captured as assumptions for fair value, future revenue, earnings and profit margins, then linked straight to a forecast and a fair value estimate. On Simply Wall St, within the Community page used by millions of investors, Narratives are an accessible tool that helps you compare your Fair Value to the current price, which can guide your decision on whether the stock looks attractive or expensive to you. Narratives update when new information such as news or earnings is added, so your view can stay aligned with fresh data instead of a one off model. For Leidos, one investor might build a Narrative that supports a very high fair value, while another could use more cautious assumptions and arrive at a far lower number, and you can see both side by side.

Do you think there's more to the story for Leidos Holdings? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: IBM’s Open Source And AI Push Weighed Against Valuation And Debt Risks
data.description:
• Over the past week, International Business Machines (NYSE:IBM) joined more than 100 organizations in supporting Open Invention Network's expanded OIN 2.0 patent protection and funding model for open source software.
  • IBM announced new agentic AI collaborations with global partners, including e&, focused on real world use cases such as compliance and operational efficiency.
  • The company also launched AI driven digital experiences for the GRAMMYs and introduced Enterprise Advantage, a generative AI consulting service for clients building internal AI platforms.

For investors watching enterprise technology, these moves offer a snapshot of how IBM is positioning itself across open source, AI software, and consulting services. The company operates at the intersection of infrastructure, software, and services, and these updates highlight IBM's focus on AI tools designed for regulated and large scale environments.

Open source risk management, enterprise grade generative AI, and industry specific digital experiences are themes many large corporates are working through right now. IBM's latest announcements provide additional data points on how NYSE:IBM is competing for those budgets and partnerships, and how its role in ecosystems like OIN and high profile events like the GRAMMYs may influence client engagement over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for International Business Machines by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on International Business Machines.

How International Business Machines stacks up against its biggest competitors

Advertisement

  • ⚖️ Price vs Analyst Target: At US$293.86 versus a consensus target of about US$305.16, IBM trades roughly 4% below analyst expectations, which sits inside the fair value band.
  • ⚖️ Simply Wall St Valuation: Simply Wall St flags IBM as trading close to its estimated fair value, so the current price does not screen as a clear bargain or as stretched.
  • ❌ Recent Momentum: The 30 day return of about 3.7% decline shows recent weakness even as the company pushes new AI and open source initiatives.

Check out Simply Wall St's in depth valuation analysis for International Business Machines.

Key Considerations

  • 📊 OIN 2.0 participation and new AI offerings reinforce IBM's role in open source and enterprise AI, which could be relevant for how you think about its IT and software positioning.
  • 📊 With a P/E of about 34.7 versus an industry average of roughly 29.3, investors may want to watch whether AI collaborations and generative AI services support that premium.
  • ⚠️ IBM carries a flagged risk around a high level of debt, which is important when assessing the cost and payoff of long term AI and platform investments.

data.title: Is Chewy (CHWY) Pricing Reflect Recent Share Weakness And DCF Versus P/E Signals
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Chewy's current share price lines up with its true value, this article is designed to walk you through that question step by step.
  • Chewy's shares last closed at US$31.30, with returns of a 5.1% decline over 7 days, a 3.7% decline over 30 days, a 6.5% decline year to date, a 19.5% decline over 1 year, a 30.5% decline over 3 years, and a 69.2% decline over 5 years. These figures may affect how investors are thinking about both risk and potential upside.
  • Recent headlines around Chewy have focused on how the business is positioning itself within the online pet retail space, and how its customer relationships and subscription style offerings fit into that picture. This backdrop provides useful context when you weigh up the share price moves against the underlying business story.
  • On our checks, Chewy scores 3 out of 6 on valuation, as shown by its value score of 3. We will walk through what different valuation methods say about that number and finish with a way to look at valuation that can give you a fuller picture than any single metric alone.

Find out why Chewy's -19.5% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Chewy Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model takes estimates of the cash a business could generate in the future and discounts those amounts back to today. This provides a single estimate of what the equity might be worth per share right now.

For Chewy, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $476.8 million. Analysts, together with Simply Wall St extrapolations beyond the analyst horizon, project annual free cash flow reaching $1,240.0 million by 2030, with further projections out to 2035. All of these cash flows are in US$, then discounted back to today using the DCF framework.

Putting those projections together, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $56.48 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $31.30, this model suggests the stock screens as around 44.6% undervalued.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Chewy is undervalued by 44.6%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 876 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Chewy.

Approach 2: Chewy Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It lets you quickly compare how the market is pricing those earnings relative to other businesses.

What counts as a "normal" P/E depends a lot on two things: growth expectations and risk. Higher expected earnings growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually lines up with a lower one.

Chewy currently trades on a P/E of 62.92x. That sits above the Specialty Retail industry average of 20.05x and the peer group average of 25.65x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary Fair Ratio of 28.50x, which is the P/E it would expect for Chewy given factors like its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics.

This Fair Ratio can be more useful than a simple peer or industry comparison because it aims to adjust for differences in growth, risk, profitability, size and sector, rather than assuming all retailers should trade on the same multiple. Comparing Chewy's current P/E of 62.92x with the Fair Ratio of 28.50x suggests the shares screen as overvalued on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1422 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Chewy Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives. With Narratives, you attach a clear story about Chewy to the numbers you think are realistic for its future revenue, earnings and margins.

A Narrative is simply your view of the business written into a forecast, then linked directly to an estimated fair value per share. This way, you are not just looking at ratios in isolation, but at how Chewy’s business story could play out in its financials.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, used by millions of investors, you can browse and create these Narratives, compare fair values with the current Chewy share price, and use that gap to help you decide whether you see conditions as closer to a buy, a hold, or a sell.

Narratives also update automatically when fresh information such as earnings releases or news is added to the platform. You might see one Chewy Narrative that assumes a higher fair value based on stronger long term growth and margins, while another assumes a lower fair value based on more cautious revenue expectations and thinner margins.

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Discovery Silver (TSX:DSV) After The Recent Share Price Surge
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Discovery Silver's recent share price run still leaves room for value, this article walks through what the numbers actually say about the stock.
  • The share price closed at C$11.48, with returns of 14.1% over the last 7 days, 30.8% over the last 30 days, 36.3% year to date, and very large gains over 1 year, 3 years and 5 years that are all several times the starting level.
  • These price moves have put Discovery Silver firmly on many investors' radar. Recent company updates and sector news have added more attention to the story. This backdrop makes it a useful time to separate sentiment from what can be supported by cash flows, assets and peer comparisons.
  • Simply Wall St currently gives Discovery Silver a valuation score of 0 out of 6. In the next sections we will look at how different valuation methods arrive at that view and finish with a practical framework you can use to judge value for yourself.

Discovery Silver scores just 0/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Discovery Silver Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes estimates of the cash a business could generate in the future and discounts those amounts back to today, to arrive at an estimate of what the company might be worth now.

For Discovery Silver, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is reported at $69.53 million. Analyst and extrapolated projections used by Simply Wall St show annual free cash flow data points around $214 million for 2026 and 2027, with a series of forecasts running out to 2035, each discounted back to present value.

Putting all of those discounted cash flows together, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of $7.52 per share. Compared with the current share price of C$11.48, the model suggests the stock is 52.6% overvalued according to these assumptions.

On this cash flow view, the market price sits well above the modelled value.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Discovery Silver may be overvalued by 52.6%. Discover 876 undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Discovery Silver.

Approach 2: Discovery Silver Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings can be volatile, using the P/S ratio can be a useful way to compare what investors are paying for each dollar of revenue rather than each dollar of profit.

In general, higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk can support a higher P/S multiple. In contrast, slower growth or higher risk usually go with a lower, more conservative range. So the question is whether Discovery Silver’s current P/S lines up with what you might consider normal for its profile.

Discovery Silver currently trades on a P/S of 18.02x. That sits above the Metals and Mining industry average of 9.30x and also above the peer group average of 15.79x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio model, which estimates what a more appropriate multiple could be after considering factors such as earnings growth, industry, profit margin, market cap and risks, arrives at a P/S of 10.45x.

This Fair Ratio can often be more informative than a straight peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for company specific characteristics instead of assuming all miners deserve the same multiple. Compared with the current 18.02x, the Fair Ratio of 10.45x points to Discovery Silver trading at a richer level than this framework implies.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/S ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1422 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Discovery Silver Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, a simple way for you to write the story behind your numbers, including your own view of fair value and future revenue, earnings and margins for Discovery Silver.

A Narrative connects three pieces in one place: what you think is happening with the business, how that translates into a financial forecast, and what you believe is a reasonable value per share based on those assumptions.

On Simply Wall St, within the Community page used by millions of investors, Narratives are an accessible tool that let you compare your Fair Value to the current share price, so you can decide for yourself whether the gap between Price and Fair Value suggests it might be a time to buy, hold, or sell.

Narratives are also refreshed when new information such as news or earnings is added to the platform. You might see one Discovery Silver Narrative that assumes a relatively low fair value alongside another that uses much higher revenue or margin expectations to arrive at a much higher fair value, reflecting how different investors can read the same company in very different ways.

Do you think there's more to the story for Discovery Silver? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Alexander's (ALX) After A 36% One Year Rally?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Alexander's current share price still offers value, it helps to step back and look at what the recent returns and fundamentals are really telling you.
  • The stock most recently closed at US$239.55, with a 7 day return of 0.5% decline, a 30 day return of 9.8%, year to date return of 9.5%, and a 1 year return of 36.3%, which naturally raises questions about how much of this is already reflected in the price.
  • These moves sit alongside a 3 year return of 27.7% and a 5 year return of 28.7%, which give extra context for anyone thinking about how the story has played out over time. While short term swings can grab attention, this broader record is often what investors look at when judging whether recent enthusiasm or caution is justified.
  • Even with that backdrop, Alexander's currently has a valuation score of 1 out of 6. Next, we will look at what different valuation methods say about that score and then finish with a framework that can help you make even more sense of it.

Alexander's scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Alexander's Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A DCF model estimates what a company could be worth by projecting its future adjusted funds from operations, then discounting those cash flows back into today's dollars.

For Alexander's, the model uses a 2 stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on adjusted funds from operations. The latest twelve month free cash flow is reported at $77.97 million. Analyst input and extrapolated estimates point to projected free cash flow of $59.10 million in 2026 and $61.00 million in 2027, with further figures extended out across 10 years using Simply Wall St assumptions.

When all those projected cash flows are discounted back and aggregated, the DCF output suggests an intrinsic value of about $173.49 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $239.55, this model indicates that Alexander's is around 38.1% overvalued.

In short, the cash flow based valuation indicates that the current price reflects a significant amount of optimism.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Alexander's may be overvalued by 38.1%. Discover 876 undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Alexander's.

Approach 2: Alexander's Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Alexander's, the P/E ratio is a useful way to relate what you pay for each share to the earnings that support that price. Investors usually accept a higher P/E when they expect stronger growth or see less risk, and a lower P/E when growth expectations or perceived risk are more modest.

Alexander's currently trades on a P/E of 33.36x. That sits above the Retail REITs industry average of about 27.11x, yet below the peer group average of 45.68x. To go a step further, Simply Wall St estimates a Fair Ratio of 27.34x. This Fair Ratio is a proprietary view of what a more suitable P/E could be, based on factors such as Alexander's earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market capitalization and specific risks.

Because the Fair Ratio blends these fundamentals, it can often be more useful than a simple comparison with industry or peer averages, which may not reflect a company's particular risk and growth mix. With the current P/E of 33.36x sitting above the Fair Ratio of 27.34x, this approach suggests the shares are trading on a richer earnings multiple than those fundamentals alone might support.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1422 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Alexander's Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, which simply means putting a clear story behind the numbers you see on Alexander's valuation and forecasts.

A Narrative is your view of how a company might perform, linked directly to assumptions for future revenue, earnings and margins, and then to a fair value that you can compare with the current share price.

On Simply Wall St, Narratives sit in the Community page and are designed to be easy to use. You can see how different stories about Alexander's translate into different forecasts and fair values, and how that might guide your own decisions about whether the gap between Fair Value and Price looks attractive or not.

Narratives also update when new information comes in, like fresh earnings or company news. You might see one investor with a very cautious view of Alexander's fair value alongside another who has a much higher fair value because they expect a very different future for the business.

data.title: Is Centene (CNC) Pricing Reflect Recent Managed Care Concerns Or Present An Opportunity
data.description:
• If you are looking at Centene and wondering whether the current share price reflects its underlying value, this article will walk through the key numbers that matter most.
  • The stock closed at US$41.53, with a 7 day return of 9.2% decline, a 30 day return of 2.5%, a year to date return of 0.6% decline, and a 1 year return of 35.8% decline, which may change how you think about both risk and upside.
  • Recent news around Centene has focused on its position in managed care and how investors are reassessing companies in the broader healthcare space. This context helps explain why the share price has moved, as the market reacts to shifting expectations for health insurers and reimbursement trends.
  • Against that backdrop, Centene currently has a valuation score of 5/6, reflecting where it screens as undervalued across several checks. Next we will look at how different valuation approaches line up for Centene, and then finish with a way of thinking about valuation that goes beyond any single model.

Find out why Centene's -35.8% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Centene Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes the cash Centene is expected to generate in the future, then discounts those projections back into today’s dollars to estimate what the business might be worth right now.

Centene’s latest twelve month free cash flow is about $3.24b. Using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, analysts and extrapolated estimates project free cash flow reaching $4.04b by 2030, with interim yearly projections between 2026 and 2035 discounted back to today. Simply Wall St uses analyst inputs for the earlier years and then extends the trend beyond the typical 5 year forecast window.

When all of those discounted cash flows are added up, the model points to an estimated intrinsic value of about $214.03 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $41.53, this implies the stock appears around 80.6% undervalued on this DCF view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Centene is undervalued by 80.6%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 876 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Approach 2: Centene Price vs Sales

For profitable companies that generate meaningful revenue, the P/S ratio is a straightforward way to connect what you are paying for each share with the sales the business produces. It is especially useful when earnings are affected by accounting items or near term factors that make P/E less informative.

What investors usually see as a “normal” or “fair” P/S depends on what they expect for future growth and how risky they believe the business is. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can support a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher uncertainty often line up with a lower one.

Centene currently trades on a P/S ratio of 0.12x, compared with a Healthcare industry average of 1.27x and a peer average of 1.57x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio framework estimates a P/S of 0.88x for Centene, based on factors such as its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics.

This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple comparison with peers or the sector because it adjusts for Centene’s own growth outlook, risk and profitability rather than assuming it should trade in line with a broad average. Since the current 0.12x P/S is well below the 0.88x Fair Ratio, the shares screen as undervalued on this metric.

Result: UNDERVALUED

A Narrative connects three pieces: the business story you believe, the financial forecast that follows from that story, and the fair value that drops out of those numbers. On Simply Wall St, Narratives sit inside the Community page, so you can quickly plug in your assumptions and see how your fair value for Centene compares with the current share price, which may help you decide whether it looks attractive or stretched. data.title: Should Air Europa’s 40-A350 Shift Reshape the Long-Haul Case for Airbus (ENXTPA:AIR) Investors?
data.description:
• Air Europa has finalized a firm order for up to 40 Airbus A350-900 widebody aircraft, cementing a shift of its long-haul fleet away from Boeing toward Airbus and anchoring its future Spain–Latin America operations.
  • The deal reinforces Airbus’s position in the long-haul widebody market, highlighting airline preference for newer aircraft families that combine range, cargo capability, and passenger comfort on key intercontinental routes.
  • We’ll now examine how Air Europa’s A350-900 commitment, and Airbus’s strengthening role in long-haul fleets, shapes the company’s investment narrative.

Explore 23 top quantum computing companies leading the revolution in next-gen technology and shaping the future with breakthroughs in quantum algorithms, superconducting qubits, and cutting-edge research.

Advertisement

What Is Airbus' Investment Narrative?

To own Airbus, you have to believe the company can keep converting a huge order backlog into profitable deliveries while managing supply chain friction, engine issues and tariff uncertainty. The firm’s improving margins, double‑digit earnings growth and high return on equity show that, so far, it has been executing against that backdrop, and the share price still sits below both analyst and discounted cash flow fair value estimates. The newly firmed Air Europa order for up to 40 A350‑900s fits neatly into that story: it deepens Airbus’s role in long‑haul fleets and supports the A350 production ramp, but it is incremental rather than transformational against an already very large backlog, so it is unlikely to change the near term earnings catalysts on its own. Where it might matter more is in reinforcing Airbus’s competitive position in the twin‑aisle market at a time when investors are already watching execution risk closely.

However, the same backlog that supports growth also magnifies any slip in deliveries that investors should be aware of. Despite retreating, Airbus' shares might still be trading 29% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Nineteen fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly €169,921.30 to €280,019.55, underlining how differently people are thinking about Airbus. Set that against the current focus on delivery execution and geopolitical risk, and it becomes clear why it helps to weigh several contrasting views before deciding how this stock fits into your portfolio.

data.title: Does FDA’s No-New-Trial Stance on Deramiocel Shift the Bull Case for Capricor Therapeutics (CAPR)?
data.description:
• Capricor Therapeutics recently issued a regulatory update on its Biologics License Application for Deramiocel in Duchenne muscular dystrophy, confirming that the FDA has requested the full Phase 3 HOPE-3 clinical study report and supporting data, but no new clinical trials or patient data.
  • The absence of a request for additional studies, combined with Capricor’s plan to submit the HOPE-3 report in February 2026, places the focus squarely on data interpretation and the potential for a new PDUFA action timeline.
  • We’ll now examine how the FDA’s decision not to require new Deramiocel trials shapes Capricor’s investment narrative and future risk profile.

This technology could replace computers: discover 23 stocks that are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

What Is Capricor Therapeutics' Investment Narrative?

For anyone considering Capricor, the big picture still revolves around a simple belief: that Deramiocel can become a meaningful therapy in Duchenne muscular dystrophy and ultimately support a commercial business that justifies today’s valuation swings. The latest FDA update, asking for the full HOPE-3 study report but not new trials, keeps the key near term catalyst squarely tied to how regulators interpret existing data and whether a new PDUFA date is set after the planned February 2026 submission. That is material for the story, because it shifts the focus from clinical risk to regulatory and timing risk. At the same time, the company remains unprofitable, dependent on external funding, and exposed to share price volatility, so execution around the HOPE-3 package and the Nippon Shinyaku partnership matters more than ever.

However, the biggest risk many investors might be underestimating sits squarely on the regulatory side. Despite retreating, Capricor Therapeutics' shares might still be trading above their fair value and there could be some more downside. Discover how much.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The Simply Wall St Community’s seven fair value estimates for Capricor span US$16.49 to about US$256.25, reflecting very different views on upside. Set this against the recent FDA request for more HOPE-3 detail, and you can see why opinions on future performance and risk are so far apart.

data.title: Cornerstone Bancorp (OTCPK:CNBP) EPS Growth Outpaces Long Term Trend And Fuels Bullish Narrative
data.description:
Cornerstone Bancorp (CNBP) closed out FY 2025 with fourth quarter total revenue of US$11.0 million and basic EPS of US$3.14, alongside net income of US$3.09 million that capped off a year of steady profitability. The company has seen quarterly revenue move from US$9.66 million in Q4 FY 2024 to US$11.0 million in Q4 FY 2025, while basic EPS over that same period went from US$3.02 to US$3.14, supported by a trailing twelve month net profit margin of 25.9% versus 25.7% a year earlier. Taken together, these numbers point to a business that is holding its margins and giving investors a clear, earnings driven story to assess this latest report.

See our full analysis for Cornerstone Bancorp.

With the headline figures on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up against the widely followed narratives around Cornerstone Bancorp’s growth, quality, and risk.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

TTM earnings growth running at 10.9%

  • Over the last 12 months, Cornerstone Bancorp generated trailing net income of US$10.821 million and basic EPS of US$11.00, with earnings growth of 10.9% versus a 5 year average of 2.4% per year.
  • What stands out for the more bullish narrative is how the 10.9% recent earnings growth and a trailing net profit margin of 25.9% line up with the idea of a steady community bank, yet
    • the five year earnings growth rate of 2.4% per year is much more moderate, so the latest year looks stronger than the longer trend,
    • and quarterly EPS stepping from US$2.44 in Q1 FY 2025 to US$3.14 in Q4 FY 2025 shows a consistent run through the year that supports those bullish arguments about earnings resilience.
Have recent earnings strength really changed the story, or is CNBP still the same slow and steady bank investors expect? 📊 Read the full Cornerstone Bancorp Consensus Narrative.

P/E of 13.6x versus richer peers

  • The shares trade on a trailing P/E of 13.6x, above the peer average of 12.7x and the US Banks industry average of 11.8x, even though the trailing net profit margin only moved slightly from 25.7% to 25.9% over the past year.
  • Skeptical or more bearish views often point to valuation, and that concern is anchored here because
    • the higher P/E multiple means investors are already paying more per dollar of trailing earnings than for the average peer or the broader US Banks group,
    • while the modest change in net profit margin suggests only a small improvement in profitability to support that richer multiple.

DCF fair value above US$221 versus US$150 price

  • The DCF fair value of US$221.20 sits well above the current share price of US$150.00, which implies the stock is about 32.2% below that modelled value even as earnings have grown 10.9% over the last year.
  • Supporters of the more bullish view see this valuation gap as important because
    • the trailing 12 month revenue of US$41.818 million and net income of US$10.821 million show a consistently profitable profile that feeds into the cash flow assumptions behind that DCF fair value,
    • yet the shares being described as highly illiquid over the past three months means that even with this gap, trading can be harder and price moves may not always track the fundamentals closely.

Next Steps

Don't just look at this quarter; the real story is in the long-term trend. We've done an in-depth analysis on Cornerstone Bancorp's growth and its valuation to see if today's price is a bargain. Add the company to your watchlist or portfolio now so you don't miss the next big move.

See What Else Is Out There

Cornerstone Bancorp’s richer 13.6x P/E multiple against peers and only slightly higher profit margin leave some investors questioning how much upside is already priced in.

data.title: Is InMode Ltd.'s (NASDAQ:INMD) Recent Stock Performance Tethered To Its Strong Fundamentals?
data.description:
InMode (NASDAQ:INMD) has had a great run on the share market with its stock up by a significant 10% over the last week. Since the market usually pay for a company’s long-term fundamentals, we decided to study the company’s key performance indicators to see if they could be influencing the market. Particularly, we will be paying attention to InMode's ROE today.

Return on equity or ROE is a key measure used to assess how efficiently a company's management is utilizing the company's capital. In short, ROE shows the profit each dollar generates with respect to its shareholder investments.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

How To Calculate Return On Equity?

ROE can be calculated by using the formula:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for InMode is:

23% = US$150m ÷ US$653m (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

The 'return' refers to a company's earnings over the last year. Another way to think of that is that for every $1 worth of equity, the company was able to earn $0.23 in profit.

See our latest analysis for InMode

What Is The Relationship Between ROE And Earnings Growth?

So far, we've learned that ROE is a measure of a company's profitability. Based on how much of its profits the company chooses to reinvest or "retain", we are then able to evaluate a company's future ability to generate profits. Assuming everything else remains unchanged, the higher the ROE and profit retention, the higher the growth rate of a company compared to companies that don't necessarily bear these characteristics.

InMode's Earnings Growth And 23% ROE

To start with, InMode's ROE looks acceptable. Further, the company's ROE compares quite favorably to the industry average of 9.8%. This certainly adds some context to InMode's decent 10.0% net income growth seen over the past five years.

As a next step, we compared InMode's net income growth with the industry and found that the company has a similar growth figure when compared with the industry average growth rate of 12% in the same period.

Earnings growth is a huge factor in stock valuation. The investor should try to establish if the expected growth or decline in earnings, whichever the case may be, is priced in. By doing so, they will have an idea if the stock is headed into clear blue waters or if swampy waters await. One good indicator of expected earnings growth is the P/E ratio which determines the price the market is willing to pay for a stock based on its earnings prospects. So, you may want to check if InMode is trading on a high P/E or a low P/E, relative to its industry.

Is InMode Efficiently Re-investing Its Profits?

InMode doesn't pay any regular dividends, meaning that all of its profits are being reinvested in the business, which explains the fair bit of earnings growth the company has seen.

Summary

Overall, we are quite pleased with InMode's performance. Particularly, we like that the company is reinvesting heavily into its business, and at a high rate of return. Unsurprisingly, this has led to an impressive earnings growth. That being so, according to the latest industry analyst forecasts, the company's earnings are expected to shrink in the future. To know more about the latest analysts predictions for the company, check out this visualization of analyst forecasts for the company.

data.title: Olympus (TSE:7733) Has Announced That It Will Be Increasing Its Dividend To ¥30.00
data.description:
Olympus Corporation (TSE:7733) will increase its dividend from last year's comparable payment on the 5th of June to ¥30.00. This will take the annual payment to 1.6% of the stock price, which is above what most companies in the industry pay.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

Olympus' Future Dividend Projections Appear Well Covered By Earnings

We like to see robust dividend yields, but that doesn't matter if the payment isn't sustainable. However, based ont he last payment, Olympus was earning enough to cover the dividend pretty comfortably. However, with more than 75% of free cash flow being paid out to shareholders, future growth could potentially be constrained.

Over the next year, EPS is forecast to expand by 12.0%. If the dividend continues along recent trends, we estimate the payout ratio will be 35%, which is in the range that makes us comfortable with the sustainability of the dividend.

Check out our latest analysis for Olympus

Olympus Has A Solid Track Record

The company has a sustained record of paying dividends with very little fluctuation. The dividend has gone from an annual total of ¥2.50 in 2016 to the most recent total annual payment of ¥30.00. This implies that the company grew its distributions at a yearly rate of about 28% over that duration. We can see that payments have shown some very nice upward momentum without faltering, which provides some reassurance that future payments will also be reliable.

The Dividend Looks Likely To Grow

Investors who have held shares in the company for the past few years will be happy with the dividend income they have received. It's encouraging to see that Olympus has been growing its earnings per share at 22% a year over the past five years. Earnings per share is growing at a solid clip, and the payout ratio is low which we think is an ideal combination in a dividend stock as the company can quite easily raise the dividend in the future.

In Summary

Overall, it's great to see the dividend being raised and that it is still in a sustainable range. However, lack of cash flows makes us wary of the potential for cuts in the dividend's future, even though the dividend is generally looking okay. Taking all of this into consideration, the dividend looks viable moving forward, but investors should be mindful that the company has pushed the boundaries of sustainability in the past and may do so again.

It's important to note that companies having a consistent dividend policy will generate greater investor confidence than those having an erratic one. However, there are other things to consider for investors when analysing stock performance. Companies that are growing earnings tend to be the best dividend stocks over the long term. See what the 13 analysts we track are forecasting for Olympus for free with public analyst estimates for the company. If you are a dividend investor, you might also want to look at our curated list of high yield dividend stocks.

data.title: OBICLtd (TSE:4684) Is Paying Out A Larger Dividend Than Last Year
data.description:
OBIC Co.,Ltd.'s (TSE:4684) dividend will be increasing from last year's payment of the same period to ¥47.00 on 29th of June. This will take the annual payment to 1.9% of the stock price, which is above what most companies in the industry pay.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

OBICLtd's Payment Could Potentially Have Solid Earnings Coverage

Impressive dividend yields are good, but this doesn't matter much if the payments can't be sustained. Prior to this announcement, OBICLtd's dividend was comfortably covered by both cash flow and earnings. This indicates that a lot of the earnings are being reinvested into the business, with the aim of fueling growth.

Looking forward, earnings per share is forecast to rise by 7.7% over the next year. If the dividend continues on this path, the payout ratio could be 54% by next year, which we think can be pretty sustainable going forward.

View our latest analysis for OBICLtd

Dividend Volatility

The company's dividend history has been marked by instability, with at least one cut in the last 10 years. Since 2016, the dividend has gone from ¥14.00 total annually to ¥84.00. This implies that the company grew its distributions at a yearly rate of about 20% over that duration. Dividends have grown rapidly over this time, but with cuts in the past we are not certain that this stock will be a reliable source of income in the future.

The Dividend Looks Likely To Grow

Growing earnings per share could be a mitigating factor when considering the past fluctuations in the dividend. OBICLtd has impressed us by growing EPS at 15% per year over the past five years. Since earnings per share is growing at an acceptable rate, and the payout policy is balanced, we think the company is positioning itself well to grow earnings and dividends in the future.

We Really Like OBICLtd's Dividend

Overall, a dividend increase is always good, and we think that OBICLtd is a strong income stock thanks to its track record and growing earnings. The company is easily earning enough to cover its dividend payments and it is great to see that these earnings are being translated into cash flow. Taking this all into consideration, this looks like it could be a good dividend opportunity.

It's important to note that companies having a consistent dividend policy will generate greater investor confidence than those having an erratic one. Still, investors need to consider a host of other factors, apart from dividend payments, when analysing a company. For example, we've picked out 1 warning sign for OBICLtd that investors should know about before committing capital to this stock. If you are a dividend investor, you might also want to look at our curated list of high yield dividend stocks.

data.title: Asahi Diamond Industrial (TSE:6140) shareholder returns have been impressive, earning 156% in 5 years
data.description:
The most you can lose on any stock (assuming you don't use leverage) is 100% of your money. But on a lighter note, a good company can see its share price rise well over 100%. For example, the Asahi Diamond Industrial Co., Ltd. (TSE:6140) share price has soared 119% in the last half decade. Most would be very happy with that. And in the last month, the share price has gained 25%.

After a strong gain in the past week, it's worth seeing if longer term returns have been driven by improving fundamentals.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

While the efficient markets hypothesis continues to be taught by some, it has been proven that markets are over-reactive dynamic systems, and investors are not always rational. One flawed but reasonable way to assess how sentiment around a company has changed is to compare the earnings per share (EPS) with the share price.

During the five years of share price growth, Asahi Diamond Industrial moved from a loss to profitability. That kind of transition can be an inflection point that justifies a strong share price gain, just as we have seen here.

You can see below how EPS has changed over time (discover the exact values by clicking on the image).

We know that Asahi Diamond Industrial has improved its bottom line lately, but is it going to grow revenue? Check if analysts think Asahi Diamond Industrial will grow revenue in the future.

Advertisement

What About Dividends?

As well as measuring the share price return, investors should also consider the total shareholder return (TSR). Whereas the share price return only reflects the change in the share price, the TSR includes the value of dividends (assuming they were reinvested) and the benefit of any discounted capital raising or spin-off. Arguably, the TSR gives a more comprehensive picture of the return generated by a stock. In the case of Asahi Diamond Industrial, it has a TSR of 156% for the last 5 years. That exceeds its share price return that we previously mentioned. This is largely a result of its dividend payments!

A Different Perspective

Asahi Diamond Industrial provided a TSR of 25% over the last twelve months. But that return falls short of the market. On the bright side, that's still a gain, and it's actually better than the average return of 21% over half a decade This suggests the company might be improving over time. While it is well worth considering the different impacts that market conditions can have on the share price, there are other factors that are even more important. Consider risks, for instance. Every company has them, and we've spotted 3 warning signs for Asahi Diamond Industrial you should know about.

If you would prefer to check out another company -- one with potentially superior financials -- then do not miss this free list of companies that have proven they can grow earnings.

Please note, the market returns quoted in this article reflect the market weighted average returns of stocks that currently trade on Japanese exchanges.

data.title: Asahi Diamond Industrial Co., Ltd.'s (TSE:6140) Shares Climb 25% But Its Business Is Yet to Catch Up
data.description:
The Asahi Diamond Industrial Co., Ltd. (TSE:6140) share price has done very well over the last month, posting an excellent gain of 25%. Taking a wider view, although not as strong as the last month, the full year gain of 21% is also fairly reasonable.

Following the firm bounce in price, Asahi Diamond Industrial's price-to-earnings (or "P/E") ratio of 17.4x might make it look like a sell right now compared to the market in Japan, where around half of the companies have P/E ratios below 14x and even P/E's below 10x are quite common. Although, it's not wise to just take the P/E at face value as there may be an explanation why it's as high as it is.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Recent times have been advantageous for Asahi Diamond Industrial as its earnings have been rising faster than most other companies. The P/E is probably high because investors think this strong earnings performance will continue. You'd really hope so, otherwise you're paying a pretty hefty price for no particular reason.

Check out our latest analysis for Asahi Diamond Industrial

If you'd like to see what analysts are forecasting going forward, you should check out our free report on Asahi Diamond Industrial.
Advertisement

How Is Asahi Diamond Industrial's Growth Trending?

There's an inherent assumption that a company should outperform the market for P/E ratios like Asahi Diamond Industrial's to be considered reasonable.

Taking a look back first, we see that the company grew earnings per share by an impressive 47% last year. Still, incredibly EPS has fallen 12% in total from three years ago, which is quite disappointing. So unfortunately, we have to acknowledge that the company has not done a great job of growing earnings over that time.

Shifting to the future, estimates from the two analysts covering the company suggest earnings should grow by 0.5% per annum over the next three years. That's shaping up to be materially lower than the 9.0% each year growth forecast for the broader market.

In light of this, it's alarming that Asahi Diamond Industrial's P/E sits above the majority of other companies. Apparently many investors in the company are way more bullish than analysts indicate and aren't willing to let go of their stock at any price. Only the boldest would assume these prices are sustainable as this level of earnings growth is likely to weigh heavily on the share price eventually.

What We Can Learn From Asahi Diamond Industrial's P/E?

The large bounce in Asahi Diamond Industrial's shares has lifted the company's P/E to a fairly high level. It's argued the price-to-earnings ratio is an inferior measure of value within certain industries, but it can be a powerful business sentiment indicator.

Our examination of Asahi Diamond Industrial's analyst forecasts revealed that its inferior earnings outlook isn't impacting its high P/E anywhere near as much as we would have predicted. When we see a weak earnings outlook with slower than market growth, we suspect the share price is at risk of declining, sending the high P/E lower. Unless these conditions improve markedly, it's very challenging to accept these prices as being reasonable.

We don't want to rain on the parade too much, but we did also find 3 warning signs for Asahi Diamond Industrial that you need to be mindful of.

If these risks are making you reconsider your opinion on Asahi Diamond Industrial, explore our interactive list of high quality stocks to get an idea of what else is out there.

data.title: Investors in Nippon Yusen Kabushiki Kaisha (TSE:9101) have seen incredible returns of 822% over the past five years
data.description:
Long term investing can be life changing when you buy and hold the truly great businesses. While the best companies are hard to find, but they can generate massive returns over long periods. For example, the Nippon Yusen Kabushiki Kaisha (TSE:9101) share price is up a whopping 466% in the last half decade, a handsome return for long term holders. If that doesn't get you thinking about long term investing, we don't know what will. The last week saw the share price soften some 1.5%.

With that in mind, it's worth seeing if the company's underlying fundamentals have been the driver of long term performance, or if there are some discrepancies.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

While markets are a powerful pricing mechanism, share prices reflect investor sentiment, not just underlying business performance. By comparing earnings per share (EPS) and share price changes over time, we can get a feel for how investor attitudes to a company have morphed over time.

Over half a decade, Nippon Yusen Kabushiki Kaisha managed to grow its earnings per share at 56% a year. The EPS growth is more impressive than the yearly share price gain of 41% over the same period. So it seems the market isn't so enthusiastic about the stock these days. The reasonably low P/E ratio of 6.55 also suggests market apprehension.

You can see below how EPS has changed over time (discover the exact values by clicking on the image).

Dive deeper into Nippon Yusen Kabushiki Kaisha's key metrics by checking this interactive graph of Nippon Yusen Kabushiki Kaisha's earnings, revenue and cash flow.

Advertisement

What About Dividends?

It is important to consider the total shareholder return, as well as the share price return, for any given stock. The TSR incorporates the value of any spin-offs or discounted capital raisings, along with any dividends, based on the assumption that the dividends are reinvested. It's fair to say that the TSR gives a more complete picture for stocks that pay a dividend. We note that for Nippon Yusen Kabushiki Kaisha the TSR over the last 5 years was 822%, which is better than the share price return mentioned above. This is largely a result of its dividend payments!

A Different Perspective

Nippon Yusen Kabushiki Kaisha shareholders gained a total return of 11% during the year. But that return falls short of the market. If we look back over five years, the returns are even better, coming in at 56% per year for five years. It may well be that this is a business worth popping on the watching, given the continuing positive reception, over time, from the market. I find it very interesting to look at share price over the long term as a proxy for business performance. But to truly gain insight, we need to consider other information, too. To that end, you should learn about the 2 warning signs we've spotted with Nippon Yusen Kabushiki Kaisha (including 1 which can't be ignored) .

We will like Nippon Yusen Kabushiki Kaisha better if we see some big insider buys. While we wait, check out this free list of undervalued stocks (mostly small caps) with considerable, recent, insider buying.

Please note, the market returns quoted in this article reflect the market weighted average returns of stocks that currently trade on Japanese exchanges.
